step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{B} No — Ustekinumab is explicitly classified as a "human monoclonal antibody" across several sources, indicating it is not a polyclonal antibody. Polyclonal antibodies involve multiple antigen-specific immune responses from different B cells, whereas monoclonal antibodies originate from a single clone of B cells targeting one specific epitope. Since ustekinumab targets specifically the shared p40 subunit of IL-12 and IL-23, its design aligns with monoclonal rather than polyclonal production. Therefore, option B is correct.
raw_response:
\boxed{B} No — Ustekinumab is explicitly classified as a "human monoclonal antibody" across several sources, indicating it is not a polyclonal antibody. Polyclonal antibodies involve multiple antigen-specific immune responses from different B cells, whereas monoclonal antibodies originate from a single clone of B cells targeting one specific epitope. Since ustekinumab targets specifically the shared p40 subunit of IL-12 and IL-23, its design aligns with monoclonal rather than polyclonal production. Therefore, option B is correct.
\boxed{B}
